visual_features.overall_style: Conservative, institutional school website with a corporate-government feel. Uses strong blue branding, clear typographic hierarchy, and simple flat UI with minimal decoration. Slightly dated but stable, emphasizing clarity and reliability over playfulness.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven primarily by size, weight, and background contrast. The blue global nav bar and circular school logo establish brand at the top. A large dark grey gradient banner with centered 'Open House' title acts as the page-level header. Below, a secondary tab strip highlights the current section. The event H1 in large bold grey text is the main in-content focal point, followed by a short bullet list of key details. Breadcrumbs and utility links are visually de-emphasized using smaller, lighter-grey text.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderate whitespace. Each horizontal band (utility bar, global nav, grey banner, tab strip, main content) has clear vertical padding, giving distinct separation. Inside the main content, paragraphs and bullet lists have standard line spacing and 8–16px gaps, avoiding crowding but not especially airy. The outer page margins around the centered content column are generous on desktop, adding breathing room.
unique_elements: Circular school logo with illustrated wolf and 'FCS' initials in a yellow disk on blue background
Full-width dark grey horizontal gradient banner used for the page section title
Secondary navigation as a tabbed strip with an inverted triangle marker under the active tab
Breadcrumb trail with a small home icon preceding the path

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Centered fixed-width content column likely based on a 12-column grid (~1080–1200px wide) within full-bleed horizontal bands.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic top-heavy, bar-based layout: stacked full-width horizontal strips (utility bar, global nav, page banner, secondary tabs) above a single main content column.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: From top to bottom: 1) white utility bar with contact icons and search, 2) blue global navigation with logo and main categories, 3) dark grey gradient banner with page title, 4) light tabbed sub-navigation bar, 5) breadcrumb row, 6) main content area with H1 event title, bullet list details, and paragraphs. Content is left-aligned inside a centered container.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Strong left alignment for content and navigation items; central alignment only for the page banner title. Global nav items are horizontally distributed but baseline-aligned. Vertical stacking is regular and grid-like.
responsive_hints: Full-width background bands with centered inner container suggest fluid behavior on narrower viewports.
Horizontal global nav is likely to collapse into a hamburger or stacked list on mobile.
The tabbed secondary nav uses wide, touchable horizontal targets that can stack vertically on smaller screens.
Text blocks are not excessively wide, implying max-width constraints beneficial for smaller devices.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow with clearly separated full-width sections. Above the fold, horizontal bars (utility bar, main nav, gradient banner, tabbed nav) guide the eye before reaching the main content. Within the content area, information is arranged in a single column: breadcrumb, large H1, then a short bullet list of key event facts, followed by narrative paragraphs. No sidebars are visible; any secondary content (e.g., small school logo image to the right of the H1) appears inline and right-aligned within the same horizontal row.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: On initial view, approximately the top 40% of the viewport is devoted to navigation and page framing (utility bar, main nav, banner, and tabs). The remaining 60% shows the core content starting with the large H1 and bullets. Within the content column, headings, bullets, and paragraphs occupy most of the width (around 70–80% of container), while occasional small imagery (like the right-aligned 'Fish Creek School' image) takes a minor portion. Vertical spacing between major horizontal bands is generous (~24–32px padding), while intra-content spacing is tighter (~8–16px).
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderate density. The header region feels somewhat dense due to multiple stacked bars but remains readable. The main content area has comfortable line spacing and margins, but text blocks are reasonably compact to keep information visible without excessive scrolling. Overall, it leans toward a practical, information-focused layout rather than a very spacious editorial one.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Primary flow follows an F-pattern. The eye starts at the top left with the school logo and utility icons, scans right to the Calgary Board of Education logo and search, then moves down to the blue navigation bar and reads left to right. The centered 'Open House' in the grey banner acts as a strong mid-page anchor, after which attention drops to the active tab label ('Open House') and then left to the breadcrumb and large H1. From there, the eye moves vertically through the bullet list and paragraphs.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons are not heavily featured, but interactive elements such as the tabbed navigation and potentially CTAs likely use flat rectangular shapes with slightly rounded corners (~3–4px radius). Fill colors are typically the primary blue (#005B9E) or white/light grey with a subtle 1px border (#E0E0E0). Text is medium/bold white on blue or dark grey on light backgrounds. Hover states likely use darker/lighter shade shifts and underline for text links. Icons (e.g., phone, calendar) appear inline with text, using monochrome black/grey vectors.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Most buttons are probably implemented as <a> elements styled as buttons (for navigation and CTAs) or <button> elements for forms. Structure: a single wrapper element containing optional <span class='icon'> and <span class='label'>, laid out with inline-flex or flexbox for consistent icon-text spacing.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Card-like containers are implied in the tab strip and possible content boxes: flat white rectangles with thin grey borders (#E0E0E0) separating them from the grey page background. Corner radius is subtle (0–4px). No heavy drop shadows; elevation is communicated through contrast and borders rather than shadows. Internal padding is moderate (16–24px), aligning text neatly with the grid.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical card structure would be a <div class='card'> wrapping heading, meta details, and body text. For navigation-like cards (tabs), a parent <ul> with <li> items each containing an <a> link; the active card gets a specific 'active' class that changes background and border and may add the small inverted triangle marker via CSS pseudo-element.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The visible search bar suggests flat input styling: rectangular text field with light border (#E0E0E0) and white fill, no heavy shadows, and small to medium border radius (~3px). Placeholder text is grey (#777). Search icon is a black or dark grey magnifying glass placed at the right, likely inside a button or as a positioned icon. Forms overall would be low-density, with stacked fields and labels on their own lines, reasonable vertical spacing (8–12px), and inline error feedback in red text.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Form layouts likely use stacked labels above full-width inputs within a simple <form>. Each control is grouped in a <div class='form-group'> containing <label> and <input> / <select> / <textarea>. Validation messages appear in a small <span class='error'> below the relevant input. The search bar is likely a small <form> with a text <input> and a submit <button> containing the icon.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Two navigation tiers. 1) Utility nav (top, white background): small black/grey icons with text links, horizontally spaced with ample padding and no borders; link text is dark grey with potential underline on hover. 2) Main nav (blue bar): horizontal menu with white text, evenly spaced across the bar. Items use a simple flat style; the active section may have bolder weight or a slight underline/indicator. 3) Secondary nav tabs (below banner): rectangular tabs on a light grey strip, with the active tab white and visually connected to the content area below via a darker bottom border and a small centered downward triangle marker. Inactive tabs are light grey with darker text and vertical dividers.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: All navs are likely built as <nav> elements containing <ul><li><a> structures. The main nav uses a flex or inline-block layout for horizontal distribution. The secondary nav tabs similarly use a list, with the active <li> assigned an 'active' class that adjusts background color and injects the triangle indicator via a ::after pseudo-element. Utility nav links might be individual <a> elements with icons inserted using <i> or <span> wrappers.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Lists in the main content use standard bullets with clean typography. The example bullet list has square dark bullets with left-aligned text, comfortable line height (~1.4–1.5) and 8–12px spacing between items. Email addresses and links within list items use accent color (#F28C28) and are likely underlined on hover. No additional separators; the whitespace around items and clear bullet style provide separation.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Implemented as semantic

  • elements. Optional nested for links within the text. CSS defines list-style-type (disc or square) and margin-left for indentation. For other list-based content such as event listings or announcements, a 'media object' pattern using flexbox (
  • with optional thumbnail and text wrapper) is plausible within the same list structure.

heading.visual_style: Neutral sans-serif with slightly condensed appearance, medium-to-bold weights, and simple, unobtrusive letterforms—corporate and highly legible rather than playful.

body.visual_style: Clean, humanist sans-serif suitable for long-form reading, with good x-height and open counters, similar to institutional government sites.

spacing_system.component_padding: Most interactive components (tabs, nav items, cards) use 8–16px horizontal padding and 8–12px vertical padding, generally adhering to multiples of the 8px base unit.
spacing_system.section_margin: Major horizontal bands (header, banner, tabs, main content sections) are separated by approximately 24–32px of vertical spacing, with content paragraphs and lists spaced by 8–16px.

interaction_hints: Text links likely change color and/or gain an underline on hover, particularly in the nav bars and content body.
Navigation items (global nav and tabs) probably change background shade and text weight on hover, with the active tab maintaining a white background and triangle indicator.
Buttons and clickable icons may use simple color transitions (e.g., darker blue on hover) with 150–250ms ease-in-out transitions for background-color and border-color.
Breadcrumb links likely change color on hover and may use a subtle underline to indicate clickability.
Micro-interactions are minimal and utilitarian: focus outlines on form fields, simple state changes rather than elaborate animations, supporting accessibility and clarity.
